steps.title: Identify the clue that the blank should express a question
steps.content: The phrase “Michel was determined to find out” indicates the blank should pose the question Michel wanted to answer—whether blueberries could thrive in alkaline soil with iron-solubilizing grasses.

steps.title: Apply the conventions for question formation
steps.content: A proper question uses a question mark (not a period or nothing) and inverted word order (verb before subject). “Could the blueberries thrive?” follows both conventions. The other choices either use wrong punctuation (A uses a period; B uses none, creating a run-on) or apply statement word order with question punctuation (C). That matches choice D — D is correct.
